issue_title: Preserving inline tables
issue_body:
Hey @uiri, are there any plans to support preserving inline tables in the `dumps` function. If not would you be open to a PR implementing such functionality?

Right now, I have a project with users supplying TOML, often using the inline tables syntax:

`value = {extra = ["values",]}`

While `loads` handles this just fine, serializing back to TOML creates a subsection. This is creating maintainability problems, in some cases, since things are moving around in the TOML file unexpectedly. It would be nice if the data structure was written back out as it was ingested, if possible.

Thanks for your consideration!

issue_title: Multiple configs for the same backend type (e.g. 2 XMPP configs)
issue_body:
It'd make sense to allow multiple configurations for the same backend type in your config file. I propose doing this by adding a special `backend` parameter to options that is used instead of the name listed in the `backends` option and the backend's top level key. For example, to have a configs that send to different devices in pushover:

```
---
backends:
    - pushover
pushover:
    - user_key: t0k3n
    - device: firefox
cell:
    - user_key: t0k3n
    - device: nexus5x
    - backend: pushover
```

Then `ntfy send foobar` would send a notification to all your pushover devices, where as `ntfy -b cell send foobar` would only go to the device named `nexus5x` using pushover.

cc @danryder

issue_title: Ignore warnings always with "--error", when (re-)enabled in comments
issue_body:
When using `vint --error` the following should not trigger a warning later in the code:

```vim
" vint: -ProhibitUnnecessaryDoubleQuote
let foo = "bar"
" vint: +ProhibitUnnecessaryDoubleQuote
let foo = "bar"
```

Using `--error` should really only report errors.

While at it, I think it would be useful to have a shortcut that can be used to disable a warning just for one line (the next one or the line where it is added as a comment, à la `" noqa`.

Ref: https://github.com/neomake/neomake/pull/575/commits/17dbbeee4143d626722a66591e6e869d3d88cadd

issue_title: bug in Multiply
issue_body:
First thx a lot for your great job which saved me a lot time
But I may found a big bug in your work
In your code:
```
def _augment_images(self, images, random_state, parents, hooks):
        input_dtypes = meta.copy_dtypes_for_restore(images)

        result = images
        nb_images = len(images)
        seeds = random_state.randint(0, 10**6, (nb_images,))
        for i in sm.xrange(nb_images):
            image = images[i].astype(np.float16)
            rs_image = ia.new_random_state(seeds[i])
            per_channel = self.per_channel.draw_sample(random_state=rs_image)
            if per_channel == 1:
                nb_channels = image.shape[2]
                samples = self.mul.draw_samples((nb_channels,), random_state=rs_image)
                for c, sample in enumerate(samples):
                    ia.do_assert(sample >= 0)
                    image[..., c] *= sample
                result[i] = image
            else:
                sample = self.mul.draw_sample(random_state=rs_image)
                ia.do_assert(sample >= 0)
                image *= sample
                
                result[i] = image

        # TODO make value range more flexible
        meta.clip_augmented_images_(result, 0, 255)
        meta.restore_augmented_images_dtypes_(result, input_dtypes)
```

You seem to cast a float16 into unit8 before 'clip_augmented_images' which usually will lead to overflow of the value

I think it should be:
```
 def _augment_images(self, images, random_state, parents, hooks):
        input_dtypes = meta.copy_dtypes_for_restore(images)

        result = images
        nb_images = len(images)
        seeds = random_state.randint(0, 10**6, (nb_images,))
        for i in sm.xrange(nb_images):
            image = images[i].astype(np.float16)
            rs_image = ia.new_random_state(seeds[i])
            per_channel = self.per_channel.draw_sample(random_state=rs_image)
            if per_channel == 1:
                nb_channels = image.shape[2]
                samples = self.mul.draw_samples((nb_channels,), random_state=rs_image)
                for c, sample in enumerate(samples):
                    ia.do_assert(sample >= 0)
                    image[..., c] *= sample
                result[i] = image
            else:
                sample = self.mul.draw_sample(random_state=rs_image)
                ia.do_assert(sample >= 0)
                image *= sample
                meta.clip_augmented_images_(image, 0, 255)
                result[i] = image

        # TODO make value range more flexible

        meta.restore_augmented_images_dtypes_(result, input_dtypes)

        return result
```

Am I right? :)

issue_title: Inconsistency between code and doc regarding traps _sigwatch and _sigunwatch
issue_body:
As the documentation says, kernel traps come in two flavors: regular, or synchronous.
From what I could understand from the code, synchronous traps are implemented by calling `ready_appendleft` when handling the trap to make sure the current task is rescheduled immediately without another task getting executed in the middle.

Both `_sigwatch` and `_sigunwatch` are said in the docs to be synchronous. However, they are implemented in the kernel, using `_reschedule_task`, allowing another task to get resumed.

issue_title: Plugin.say room parameter not user friendly
issue_body:
It didn't make sense to me how in configs we refer to rooms as strings but in `say` we need a `Room` object. The `Room` object isn't defined in the docs so the user would have to figure out how `Plugin` populates `self._available_rooms` to figure out everything works.

I propose we change the parameter to accept a room string, so going from

`self.say('the time is {} '.format(datetime.datetime.now()), room=self.available_rooms['CHEF'])`

to

`self.say('the time is {} '.format(datetime.datetime.now()), room='CHEF')`

If backwards compatibility is a big issue, we could do a typecheck to still support passing a Room object.

The code change would be simple and I'd be happy to submit a pull request.

issue_title: Proposal to prevent ambiguity in variable assignment
issue_body:
In the current API, the way data is assigned to the variables can be unclear. Although symfit check that either the first or the last dimension of the xdata needs to have the same length as the model has vars, this can be quite unreadable.Furthermore, the unpacking is done by the order in which the data is presented in this dimension, so if the user provides them in the wrong order the fit will be wrong.

Example in the current API, conserning some function defined on the grid 5 <= x < 5, 5 <= y < 5:
```python
xdata = np.arange(-5, 5, 1) 
ydata = np.arange(-5, 5, 1) 
xx, yy = np.meshgrid(xdata, ydata, sparse=False) 
xdata_coor = np.dstack((xx, yy)) 

zdata = 2.5 * xx**2 + 3.0 * yy**2

a, b = parameters('a, b')
x, y = variables('x, y')
new = a * x**2 + b * y**2

fit = Fit(new, xdata_coor, zdata) 
```

I would like to change the API so as to prevent these ambiquity and to increase readability.Proposal:

```python
xdata = np.arange(-5, 5, 1) 
ydata = np.arange(-5, 5, 1) 
xx, yy = np.meshgrid(xdata, ydata, sparse=False) 

zdata = 2.5 * xx**2 + 3.0 * yy**2

a, b = parameters('a, b')
x, y = Variable(data=xx), Variable(data=yy) # We have to prepare the objects seperately because we want to supply kwargs
new = a * x**2 + b * y**2

fit = Fit(new, zdata) 
```
This would increase readability, and remove the need for stacking of the data. However, the call to fit is less clear to me.

Alternatively,

```python
xdata = np.arange(-5, 5, 1) 
ydata = np.arange(-5, 5, 1) 
xx, yy = np.meshgrid(xdata, ydata, sparse=False) 

zdata = 2.5 * xx**2 + 3.0 * yy**2

a, b = parameters('a, b')
x, y = variables('x, y')
new = a * x**2 + b * y**2

fit = Fit(new, x=xx, y=yy, data=zdata) 
```

This way the fit object can link the keyword arguments to the corrosponding variables, and throw an error if no Variable by that name exists:

```python
fit = Fit(new, q=xx, y=yy, data=zdata) # raises TypeError: no variable by the name of 'q' exists.
```

Furthermore, it would still be possible to make the data a postional argument, but the variables would have to be keyword arguments:
```python
fit = Fit(new, zdata, x=xx, y=yy)
```

What do you think? Do you like one of these suggestions, or would you like something else? More ideas are welcome.

issue_title: LeastSquares as a seperate model type
issue_body:
Currently, `Model`'s also define a `Model.chi_squared`, `Model.chi_squared_jacobian`, and related functionality. They also define the variables which will hold the standard deviations. But conceptually, this should not be the responsibility of `Model` itself.

It is therefore worth considering making a subclass of `Model`, `LeastSquares`, which accepts a model and wraps it into a chi squared function. This hugely reduces the number of methods defined on model.

[I 170212 17:51:07 scheduler:569] scheduler starting...
[I 170212 17:51:07 app:76] webui running on 0.0.0.0:5000
[..snip...]
```

Many users, including me, will assume that this message indicates the port to specify in the browser url, e.g.: `http://localhost:25555/`. Supporting evidence: [a Google search for `web server running on port` (in quotes)](https://www.google.com/search?q=%22web+server+running+on+port%22).

From issue #309 I learned that this is the XMLRPC port, not the correct port for the browser URL. The relevant information is hidden somewhere down the log output: `webui running on 0.0.0.0:5000`.

Please consider making the initial startup message a bit more descriptive. Thank you.

issue_title: Use TRUE instead of true
issue_body:
There are a lot of places in the source code where `true` is being used. While this is widely acknowledged and works, it would be a good idea to use TRUE. [This](https://travis-ci.org/AbdealiJK/file-metadata/builds/135429131#L687) travis build fails saying `cannot convert bool to boolean for ...`

The same issue was found in [lammps](https://sourceforge.net/p/lammps/mailman/message/32775346/) and they were able to fix it by using TRUE and Axel Kohlmeyer says:

> i think that is more likely due to the compiler.
> in any case, the suggested fix is correct and compatible with all
> compiler variants that i could test.

issue_title: Keyring errors should derive from a base class
issue_body:
Quoting the python [guidance on user-defined exceptions](https://docs.python.org/3.6/tutorial/errors.html#tut-userexceptions):
> When creating a module that can raise several distinct errors, a common practice is to create a base class for exceptions defined by that module, and subclass that to create specific exception classes for different error conditions

This is useful for programs that call a module/package to identify the source of runtime errors.

issue_title: Reloading or swapping rulesets at runtime: is it possible?
issue_body:
Note: my preferred working environment is Python, but it's still early enough in my project's development cycle to change to something else.

Reading through other issues, I've seen some attempts at removing or installing additional rules by interacting directly with the rules module, as in #96 and #136, but I can't seem to find documentation for this, leading me to think it's unsupported. (The C code is easy to follow as a form of living documentation if I'm wrong, though)

What I'm hoping to do is slightly different from what I've seen before, though: create a full ruleset at runtime and replace the running ones, as a way of supporting operational exceptions without encoding all of the possible conditions into antecedents ahead of time. But it's not clear how to go about doing this: there doesn't seem to be a way to stop or pause rules execution after `run_all()` is called, and there are no locks around the rulesets list in the Python bindings, making me think that attempting to replace elements is likely not threadsafe.

Is replacing rulesets at runtime supported? If not, is there any simple way to transfer in-Redis context to a new client process so the system can restart with minimal downtime and state-loss?

issue_title: Rebalancing error: Cash management bug
issue_body:
There is a problem in rebalancing implementation (Rebalance() and its sister classes).  The cash target code goes into a infinite recursive loop if target weights are negative (short positions). (_I've also seen it happen even in a portfolio of positive weights with a commission function which causes cash to dip below_) 


"Exception: Potentially infinite loop detected. This occured while trying to reduce the amount of shares purchased to respect the outlay <= amount rule. This is most likely due to a commission function that outputs a commission that is greater than the amount of cash a short sale can raise."

############# test.py ###############
import pandas as pd
import bt
import datetime as dt
import random


date_span = pd.DatetimeIndex(start='10/1/2017', end='10/11/2017', freq='B')
numper = len(date_span.values)
comms = 0.01

data  = [[10,15,20,25,30,35,40,45],
         [10,10,10,10,20,20,20,20],
         [20,20,20,30,30,30,40,40],
         [20,10,20,10,20,10,20,10]]
data = [[row[i] for row in data] for i in range(len(data[0]))] #Transpose
price = pd.DataFrame(data=data, index=date_span)
price.columns = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd']
#price = price[['a', 'b']]

sig1 = pd.DataFrame(price['a'] >= price['b'] +10, columns=['a'])
sig2 = pd.DataFrame(price['a'] < price['b'] +10, columns=['b'])
signal = sig1.join(sig2)

signal1  = price.diff(1) > 0
signal2  = price.diff(1) < 0

tw = price.copy()
tw.set_value(tw.index,tw.columns,0)  # Initialize Set everything to 0

tw[signal1] = -1.0
tw[signal2] = 1.0

s1 = bt.Strategy('long_short', [ bt.algos.WeighTarget(tw),
                                 bt.algos.RunDaily(),
                                 bt.algos.Rebalance()])

####now we create the Backtest , commissions=(lambda q, p: abs(p * q) * comms)

issue_title: Any should match against Lists
issue_body:
On `dev` branch.

```python
from typing import Any, List
import enforce

@enforce.runtime_validation
def foo(a: Any) -> Any:
    return 10

foo([10,20])
```

will result in

```python
enforce.exceptions.RuntimeTypeError: 
  The following runtime type errors were encountered:
       Argument 'a' was not of type typing.Any. Actual type was typing.List[int].
```

`Any` should match against a `List[int]`, I believe.

issue_title: StreamingBody Issue in Record Mode
issue_body:
Today I had the problem with the following code in **record** mode:

``` python
            stdout_key = self._s3.Object(bucket_name='somebucket',
                                         key='{}/stdout'.format(location))
            result_output = stdout_key.get()['Body'].read()
```

No matter what, `result_output` was always `''`.  After some digging, I was able to determine that the stream was already read.

``` python
(Pdb) test = stdout_key.get()['Body']
(Pdb) test._raw_stream.tell()
5
```

Important note: the tests run fine and work as expected when in **playback** mode, and the content of the StreamingBody is saved correctly to the cassette.

My assumption: Placebo is `read()`ing the StreamingBody while in record mode so that it can save it to the cassette.

Is there any way we can still present the results correctly? I'd like to avoid having my tests fail in record mode and then pass during playback mode. :-P

issue_title: Integrate TEST Fleet tracker
issue_body:
Requested by Dorijan, and should be completely doable, especially when Webhooks (#88) are completed. A helper could get notified on submission, and then check with the fleet tracker if the request was for a loss from a fleet. From there the helper can respond two ways:

1. Submit a comment if the request is validated (or add a comment if the request cannot be validated).

2. Instead of a comment, actually reject non-validated requests automatically.

Whether or not to 1 or 2 could (should) be configured on a divisional basis. Alliance-level, validate everything, rejecting invalid requests, while on a squad or corp level, just comment if it was validated leaving the final request status up to those running that division.

issue_title: This does not support the Minecraft education edition level.dat files.
issue_body:
This does not support the Minecraft Education Edition level.dat files out of the box. There are two main reasons:

1.  It doesn't understand the eight byte header at the start of the file the first four bytes look like a version or marker field (in my file it is an 8). The next four bytes are the length of the file in bytes not counting the eight byte header.
1. The numbers in the file are all stored in little endian format.

I was able to read my particular file by chopping out the first 8 bits and changing this block of code:
`# Struct formats used to pack and unpack numeric values

BYTE = struct.Struct('<b')
SHORT = struct.Struct('<h')
USHORT = struct.Struct('<H')
INT = struct.Struct('<i')
LONG = struct.Struct('<q')
FLOAT = struct.Struct('<f')
DOUBLE = struct.Struct('<d')`

issue_title: race condition when using the library as non root user
issue_body:
I have an udev rule in place that allows me to use the GPIOs as a normal user

    SUBSYSTEM=="gpio", ACTION=="add", RUN="/bin/sh -c '\
      chown -R root:gpio /sys/class/gpio && chmod -R 770 /sys/class/gpio;\
      chown -R root:gpio /sys$DEVPATH && chmod -R 770 /sys$DEVPATH"

The problem is that, after writing to the /sys/class/gpio/export files, udev takes a little time to adjust the permissions, so when the library accesses the gpio file it doesn't have the permission to do so.
As a workaround I added a delay in GPIO.py (see attached patch [opi.zip](https://github.com/rm-hull/OPi.GPIO/files/1330195/opi.zip)) but I think there should be a better way .
See also https://forum.armbian.com/index.php?/topic/5225-gpio-from-python-user-space-opigpio-app/

issue_title: problem with consumer reconnection after socket failure?
issue_body:
Whilst I was testing how the kafka consumer + producer cope with network timeouts / dropping, I noticed that on-reconnection of the network the client seemingly floods a large number of new connections, which in our particular environment triggers some iptables rules which are designed to prevent connection floods. Looking at the logging it appeared to be the check_version code which connects to the brokers to guess at the right api_version to use.

Explicitly setting the api_version in the consumer config (to disable the api version probing) successfully prevents this from occurring.

issue_title: Is KafkaConsumer thread safe?
issue_body:
The question is basically in the title.

On the one hand, we had `MultiprocessConsumer` which I assume must be, but it has been deprecated in favor of `KafkaConsumer`, which leads me to think that `KafkaConsumer` also is thread safe.

On the other hand I just got this error using it:

issue_title: Don't log with ERROR severity if it's not an error condition
issue_body:
I don't think kafka-python should apply ERROR severity to log messages when reporting problems that would be retried. If a subsequent retry fixes the issue, it's not a problem anymore, which means the original isue should have been reported at "warning" severity, because there seems to be a consensus that warning describes an unusual event that may or may not lead to an error, but is not an error yet in itself: 

- https://docs.python.org/2/howto/logging.html#logging-basic-tutorial
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Syslog#Severity_level
- https://logging.apache.org/log4j/1.2/apidocs/org/apache/log4j/Level.html#WARN

Ideally, an issue that will be retried would be reported as WARNING and as ERROR when retries are exhausted. Although there are borderline situations that might require extra attention, eg. example when consumer polls for messages and something happens, no retries are set up for the failed request, but it would be retried during the next poll.

issue_title: SysvService has a subtle flaw if rc?.d directories are symlinks
issue_body:
While testing this afternoon, I noticed another small issue if a system under test uses the `SysvService` to check for service state.  This one is very subtle and didn't catch my eye right away.

https://github.com/philpep/testinfra/blob/master/testinfra/modules/service.py#L93-L98

could be changed to this which follows symlinks incase the `rc?.d` are symlinks because on some Linux machines they are indeed symlinks.  

```
diff --git a/testinfra/modules/service.py b/testinfra/modules/service.py
index 7b3b197..ad1b7e0 100644
--- a/testinfra/modules/service.py
+++ b/testinfra/modules/service.py
@@ -133,7 +133,7 @@ class SysvService(Service):
     @property
     def is_enabled(self):
         return bool(self.check_output(
-            "find /etc/rc?.d/ -name %s",
+            "find -L /etc/rc?.d/ -name %s",
             "S??" + self.name,
         ))

```

Here is an example of a Linux box with `rc?.d` symlinks.  The change above should behave the same with or without symlinks.  Without the patch, `is_enabled` would always return `False`.

`cat /etc/redhat-release`
```
CentOS release 6.9 (Final)
```

` ls -ld /etc/rc?.d`
```
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 10 Apr  2 18:48 /etc/rc0.d -> rc.d/rc0.d
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 10 Apr  2 18:48 /etc/rc1.d -> rc.d/rc1.d
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 10 Apr  2 18:48 /etc/rc2.d -> rc.d/rc2.d
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 10 Apr  2 18:48 /etc/rc3.d -> rc.d/rc3.d
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 10 Apr  2 18:48 /etc/rc4.d -> rc.d/rc4.d
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 10 Apr  2 18:48 /etc/rc5.d -> rc.d/rc5.d
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 10 Apr  2 18:48 /etc/rc6.d -> rc.d/rc6.d
```

`ls -ld /etc/rc.d`
```
drwxr-xr-x 10 root root 4096 Apr  2 18:49 /etc/rc.d
```

`ls -l /etc/rc.d`
```
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Apr  2 18:49 init.d
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Apr  2 18:49 rc0.d
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Apr  2 18:49 rc1.d

drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Apr  2 18:49 rc3.d

labelImg [IMAGE_PATH] [PRE-DEFINED CLASS FILE]

```python
def main():
    '''construct main app and run it'''
    app, _win = get_main_app(sys.argv)
    return app.exec_()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    sys.exit(main())
```

issue_title: Remove support for Python 2.6
issue_body:
Python 2.6.0 was [released in 2008](https://www.python.org/download/releases/2.6/), with the final binary release (2.6.6) in [Oct 2013](https://www.python.org/download/releases/2.6.6/). Currently it is [retired and thus unsupported](https://www.python.org/download/releases/2.6.9/).

New tests for mlaunch are written for Python 2.7, and builds are currently failing due to travis-ci testing for 2.6 and 2.7 (PR #468). Note that the tests are passing in 2.7.

I propose removing support for Python 2.6, which is way outdated anyway.

issue_title: False-positive isinstance assert after generation
issue_body:
Python version: 3.6.5
OS: macOS 10.13.5
Tested versions: 0.1.0 and 0.1.1rc2

The following [line](https://github.com/vmagamedov/grpclib/blob/master/grpclib/encoding/proto.py#L8) gives false-positive AssertionError for generated class. When I removed the assert I was able to call the server and received the proper response.

How to reproduce:
To reproduce the issue the `helloworld_pb2.py` and `helloworld_grpc.py` files from hello world example should be re-generated with `grpc_tools` command inside the current directory.

- Go to directory `grpclib/example/helloworld/`
- Run the command

      python3 -m grpc_tools.protoc -I. --python_out=. --python_grpc_out=. helloworld.proto

be aware that the command called from the same directory where `.proto` file is located

- Run `server.py`
- Call server with command

      grpcurl -plaintext -proto helloworld.proto 127.0.0.1:50051 helloworld.Greeter/UnaryUnaryGreeting

This issue prevents you to store `.proto` files aside `.py` sources.

issue_title: ATR calculation for negative price series
issue_body:
Hi Daniel,

For the price series I uploaded earlier. Something is amiss in the calculation of a simple
        self.ATR = btind.AverageTrueRange(self.data1, period=self.Daily.parms.atr_len,
                                          movav=btind.MovAv.SMA)
Where as you already have - data0 is the 5min series and data1 is the daily bar series.
I think - if there is somewhere a max or min, whose initial price value is assumed to be zero (zero assumed to be minimum price), this would produce incorrect result on all negative price series.
Basically -its the edge - the very very first transition to valid data - is where the bug would hide.
I had such a bug where the initial min price as assumed to be a very large number and the inital max was assumed to be a very small number - except I used 0.0 as very small number lol ...which caused the max function to return 0.0 every time when fed a negative price series.
Thanks for your attention in this matter.

When attempting to import this module in a test project I encountered a runtime import error regarding the urlparse module from urllib.parse (ImportError: No module named parse).

After some research I discovered that this module in urllib is Python 3 specific, and for Python 2 urlparse should be imported from the urlparse module. I've fixed this locally and it works under Python 2 and 3.

issue_title: Allow vba_extract.py to support an output file name
issue_body:
I have created a small patch that allows the user to specify an output filename when running vba_extract.py. The primary reason for this is that if I extract the VBA from two (or more) Excel sheets, in the same directory the new extracted file overwrites the old one.

I proposed
The new syntax is would be:
python vba_extract.py inputfile.xlsm outputVBA.bin

The other option would be to base the output filename on the inputfile name. For example
python vba_extract.py inputfile.xlsm 

would generate vbaProject-inputfile.bin

The code change is pretty trivial either way but seems like it would be helpful. Let me know your thoughts.

issue_title: Issue with writing numbers in float32 format
issue_body:
I am using XlsxWriter to convert .mat Files into .xlsx Files.
If the .mat File only has singleprecision numbers which are in np.float32, XlsxWriter will write them as float64 and for that it appends non existing numbers to it.
Even if I convert the float32 to float64 before I write the Excelfile, it will write these non existing numbers again.
I am using Python version 2.7.3 and XlsxWriter 0.8.1 and Numpy 1.9.1

Here is some code that demonstrates the problem:
```python
import xlsxwriter
import numpy as np

workbook = xlsxwriter.Workbook('hello.xlsx')
worksheet = workbook.add_worksheet()

arr64 = np.array([0.05, 0.1], dtype=np.float64)
arr32 = np.array([0.05, 0.1], dtype=np.float32)
arr32to64 = arr32.astype(np.float64)                #converting float32 to float64

worksheet.write('A1', arr64[0])
worksheet.write('A2', arr32[0])
worksheet.write('A3', arr32to64[0])

workbook.close()
```

issue_title: Searching a shared library fails
issue_body:
refs #155

I'm trying to access a shared library via myplex. 

```
my_server = my_account.resource(my_server_name).connect()
results = my_server.library.section(section).search() //Works, 


their_server = my_account.resource(their_server_name).connect()
results = their_server.library.section(section).search() //Bad Request, due to empty Library() 

``` 
https://github.com/pkkid/python-plexapi/blob/master/plexapi/server.py#L167 has a note that only the owner has access to the /library endpoint, so when this is called on a shared server, it returns an empty library. 
What's the recommend way to query a shared library when you are not the owner? https://github.com/pkkid/python-plexapi/issues/155#issuecomment-318649702 has a note that /library/sections works, but there's no implementation for it. 
I can submit a PR for this, but wanted to make sure it's not handled elsewhere already.

issue_title: Support new 'imply' keyword
issue_body:
With the v4.10 Linux Kernel (and commit 237e3ad0f195d8fd34f1299e45f04793832a16fc) a new "imply" keyword has been added.  Quoting from the commit message:
> The "imply" keyword is a weak version of "select" where the target
> config symbol can still be turned off, avoiding those pitfalls that come
> with the "select" keyword.
>
> This is useful e.g. with multiple drivers that want to indicate their
> ability to hook into a secondary subsystem while allowing the user to
> configure that subsystem out without also having to unset these drivers.

issue_title: ^ is used as bitwise XOR, ** should be used for powers
issue_body:
This is done wrongly in math_words.json

As an example, I'll take the input `What's seven times three to the power of eight?`

Here chatterbot does this:
```python
( 7 * ( 3 ^ 8 ) ) = 77
# Reasoning:
3 ^ 8 = 0011 ^ 1000 = 1011 = 11
7 * 11 = 77
```
Whereas it should do this:
```python
( 7 * ( 3 ** 8 ) ) = 45927
# Reasoning:
3 ** 8 = 6561
7 * 6561 = 45927
```

issue_title: redis-semaphore is not a distributed semaphore
issue_body:
README says:

> A distributed semaphore and mutex built on Redis.

This isn't true. Creating the "same" semaphore `semaphore = Semaphore(Redis(), count=1, namespace='spam')` in two different threads/processes does not result in `with semaphore: ...` having desired effect (both processes will execute the `with` block together).

It only works as intended when you create a global semaphore shared by two threads like in the [example](https://github.com/bluele/redis-semaphore/blob/f7510c3d1ee551b1f7a7af12b9ce7c52efefe995/examples/semaphore.py). Needless to say redis is not required to achieve the result redis-semaphore achieves.

issue_title: Subcircuit parameters not handled correctly
issue_body:
The modified call to subcuircuits with parameters is not implemented as far as I can tell.

The spice code I am trying to arrive at is:
```
X2 7 6 GAIN  K=50m 
.SUBCKT GAIN  1  2 K=20m
*Connections: In Out
*Parameters: K Gain
E1 2 0 1 0 {K}
.ENDS
```
I have the following Python code:
``` python
circuit = Circuit('REGULATOR2')
gain = SubCircuit('GAIN', 1, 2, 'K=20')  
gain.VCVS(1, 2, 0, 1, 0, '{K}')
circuit.subcircuit(gain)
circuit.X(2, 'GAIN', 7, 6, 'K=50m')
print(str(circuit))
```
which gives
```
.title REGULATOR2
.subckt GAIN 1 2 K=20m
E1 1 0 2 0 {K}
.ends
X2 7 6 K=50m GAIN
```
Notice the incorrect order of the name and the parameters.

From http://ngspice.sourceforge.net/docs/ngspice26-manual.pdf

2.4.3 Subcircuit Calls
General form:
```XYYYYYYY N1 <N2 N3 . . . > SUBNAM```
Examples:
```
X1 2 4 17 3 1 MULTI
```
Subcircuits are used in ngspice by specifying pseudo-elements beginning with the letter X, followed by the circuit nodes to be used in expanding the subcircuit. If you use parameters, the subcircuit call will be modified (see 2.8.3).

2.8.3 Subcircuit parameters
General form:
```
.subckt < identn> node node . . . <ident>=<value> <ident>=<value> ... 
```
The syntax of a subcircuit call (invocation) is:
General form:
```X<name> node node . . . <identn> <ident>=<value> <ident>=<value> ...```
Examples:
```X2 7 6 GAIN  K=50m ```

issue_title: Add `EWSService` helpers on `Protocol`
issue_body:
Services that inherit directly from `EWSService`, namely `ResolveNames`, `GetRooms`, `GetRoomLists` and `GetServerTimeZones`, work on a protocol instance and should have helper methods on the protocol instance. E.g:

```python
c = Configuration(...)
c.protocol.resolve_names('foo@example.com')

issue_title: Speed optimizations for wavelet hash
issue_body:
This particular line: https://github.com/JohannesBuchner/imagehash/blob/master/imagehash/__init__.py#L249

can be replaced with ```np.asarray(image)```.

Timing info for the original line:
```118 ms ± 148 µs per loop (mean ± std. dev. of 7 runs, 10 loops each)```

Timing for np.asarray:
```89.6 µs ± 97.9 ns per loop (mean ± std. dev. of 7 runs, 10000 loops each)```.

This is a significant speedup for the most expensive operation in the method. Even the wavelet decompositions and reconstructions for removing the lowest frequency take `18.4 ms ± 285 µs` and `18.4 ms ± 24.6 µs` respectively.

class CrossAppAddField(migrations.AddField):
    """
    Only way I found to migrate the model of another app.
    """
    def __init__(app_label, *args, **kwargs):
        self.app_label = app_label
        super(CrossAppAddField, self).__init__(*args, **kwargs)

    def state_forwards(self, app_label, *args, **kwargs):
        """
        Override app_label.
        """
        return super(CrossAppAddField, self).state_forwards(self.app_label, *args, **kwargs)

    def database_forwards(self, app_label, *args, **kwargs):
        """
        Override app_label.

        return super(CrossAppAddField, self).database_forwards(self.app_label, *args, **kwargs)

    def database_backwards(self, app_label, *args, **kwargs):  # pragma: no cover

        return super(CrossAppAddField, self).database_backwards(self.app_label, *args, **kwargs)

issue_title: Change to is_authenticated requires applications to enforce flask version to prevent security issues
issue_body:
I know that flask-login is not 1.0 yet and thus is allowed to make breaking changes. This is not about the fact of change itself, but a peculiar side-effect of the change of which I am not sure you were aware when making the change.

I would like to point out that the change of is_authenticated from method to property leads to code which **silently** treats unauthenticated users as authenticated if run with old flask versions.

The reason being that ``bool(foo.is_authenticated)`` is always true if ``is_authenticated`` is a method, which it is in older versions of flask-login. This should definitely be mentioned more explicitly in the documentation and possibly the changelog, to prevent application developers to run into the open knife, especially those who start using flask-login only now and are not aware of the vastly different behaviour in old versions.

Thank you for your consideration.

issue_title: port_013 is looking in comments when it should not
Can not update the object that was received with objectify method.
issue_body:
I can not save the object that was received with ``objectify`` method.
For example I save user with relation to address.

```python

from sqlalchemy import Column, String, Integer, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()


class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'user'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), nullable=False)
    addresses = relationship("Address", backref="user")


class Address(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'address'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    email_address = Column(String(50), nullable=False)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('user.id'), nullable=False)

from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker

# an Engine, which the Session will use for connection
# resources
engine = create_engine('sqlite:///:memory:')
User.__table__.create(engine)
Address.__table__.create(engine)

# create a configured "Session" class
Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)

# create a Session
session = Session()
u1 = User(name='ed', addresses=[Address(email_address='ed@ed.com')])
session.add(u1)
session.commit()

Now I get dict from user oject:

from colanderalchemy import SQLAlchemySchemaNode

issue_title: Parameters of type ViChar with is_buffer of True do not generate proper code
issue_body:
This is niDCPower_SetAttributeViInt32 from NI-DCPower's public header file:

```
   ViStatus _VI_FUNC  niDCPower_SetAttributeViInt32(
      ViSession vi,
      ViConstString channelName,
      ViAttr attribute,
      ViInt32 value);
```

This is what extracted metadata looks like, minus documentation (for readability):

```
    'SetAttributeViInt32': {
        'codegen_method': 'public',
        'returns': 'ViStatus',
        'parameters': [
            {
                'direction': 'in',
                'enum': None,
                'name': 'vi',
                'type': 'ViSession',
            },
            {
                'direction': 'in',
                'enum': None,
                'is_buffer': True,
                'name': 'channelName',
                'type': 'ViChar',
            },
            {
                'direction': 'in',
                'enum': None,
                'name': 'attributeId',
                'type': 'ViAttr',
            },
            {
                'direction': 'in',
                'enum': None,
                'name': 'attributeValue',
                'type': 'ViInt32',
            },
        ],
    },
```

This is what generated Library class says:

```
    def niDCPower_SetAttributeViInt32(self, vi, channel_name, attribute_id, attribute_value):  # noqa: N802
        with self._func_lock:
            if self.niDCPower_SetAttributeViInt32_cfunc is None:
                self.niDCPower_SetAttributeViInt32_cfunc = self._library.niDCPower_SetAttributeViInt32
                self.niDCPower_SetAttributeViInt32_cfunc.argtypes = [ViSession_ctype, ViChar_ctype, ViAttr_ctype, ViInt32_ctype]  # noqa: F405
                self.niDCPower_SetAttributeViInt32_cfunc.restype = nidcpower.python_types.ViStatus
        return self.niDCPower_SetAttributeViInt32_cfunc(vi, channel_name, attribute_id, attribute_value)
```

Specifically, when setting argtypes, argument channelName is being given a type ViChar_ctype even though the key is_buffer is set to True. That means a string cannot be passed to it at all. Note that type is mismatched with the header file (ViConstString vs ViChar) but that may be an inconsistency with the source of the extracted metadata – the CVI FP file.

The same issue likely exists in many other places.

issue_title: Rename SwaggerClient to BravadoClient?
issue_body: Since Swagger has been renamed to OpenAPI, any references to Swagger are going to fall farther out of sync. We might as well rename things to `BravadoClient` but this will result in a backwards incompatible change.

issue_title: server send timings overstated
issue_body:
In a few zipkin traces, there were instances where server send was being emitted after client receive. It seems that "server send" represents the time when zipkin logging is finished (for a root span). However, a function being traced would already be completed when zipkin logging starts.

I believe the "server send" should be timestamped at the same time as `logging_start` since `logging_start` occurs when a function ends, and waiting until logging is completed would skew the timings (and make the span appear longer than the amount of time it actually took). We could potentially add a `logging_end` field to where the current "server send" is being logged.

issue_title: Identified regression in bravado_core.spec.Spec if internally_dereference_refs is enabled
issue_body:
A regression as been introduced by https://github.com/Yelp/bravado-core/commit/d23868c9cc87f30a208765b73b88dd0c12c08cad.

Affected versions
------------------
bravado-core: 4.13.3, 4.13.4, 5.0.0 and 5.0.1

Symptoms
----------
After initialising a `bravado.client.SwaggerClient` with `internally_dereference_refs` config enabled you're not able to communicate with the service.
The traceback will look like
```
  File "/home/maci/pg/test_service/virtualenv_run/lib/python3.6/site-packages/bravado/client.py", line 243, in __call__
    self.operation, request_options, **op_kwargs)
  File "/home/maci/pg/test_service/virtualenv_run/lib/python3.6/site-packages/bravado/client.py", line 271, in construct_request
    url = operation.swagger_spec.api_url.rstrip('/') + operation.path_name
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'rstrip'
```
Motivations
-----------
While working on #263 I've ensured that resources are rebuilt in case `internally_dereference_refs` is set.
This was made in order to guarantee that all the references (on the already built resources) were stripped out, so after building the `Spec` object no references will be available.
Unfortunately, `build_resource` will save a pointer to the used `Spec` instance into the resource itself but we're using a temporary instance that was not fully initialised.
The effect of this is that a `Spec` instance that is fully dereferenced will, internally, point to an other `Spec` instance bind into `Operation` instances (the pointed `Spec` instance will be used to send requests, validate responses, etc).

How to fix this
--------------
The results of the regression is that `Spec` object could raise weird exceptions due to undefined `api_url` and uses double the memory needed (which is sad too).

To fix the issue will be sufficient to replace `build_resources(tmp_spec)` with `build_resources(swagger_spec)` but we need to ensure that at that time `_internal_spec_dict` has been updated to point toward the dereferenced version of the spec dict.

How to reproduce and test
--------------------------
```python
spec_dict = {
    "swagger": "2.0",
    "info": {"title": "Test", "version": "1.0"},
    "paths": {
        "/endpoint": {
            "get": {
                "operationId": "operation",
                "responses": {"200": {"description": "random response"}},
                "tags": ["tag"],
            }
        }
    },
}
spec = Spec.from_dict(spec_dict, config={'internally_dereference_refs': True})
spec_in_object = spec.resources['tag'].operations['operation'].swagger_spec
assert spec == spec_in_object
```

issue_title: [feature request] Delete non-empty bucket
issue_body:
When I try to delete a bucket, I get the following error message:

> botocore.exceptions.ClientError: An error occurred (BucketNotEmpty) when calling the DeleteBucket operation: The bucket you tried to delete is not empty

I know there are objects in the bucket. When I tell boto to delete a bucket, I expect the objects to be deleted too.

I understand there are circumstances where users *might* like this warning. So what I propose is an option for the `client.delete_bucket()` function, like: 

    client.delete_bucket(Bucket='mybucketname',DeleteIfNonEmpty=True)

Where `DeleteIfNonEmpty` is False by default.

Then if `DeleteIfNonEmpty` is True, boto seamlessly deletes the whole bucket, just like deleting a directory in a file browser.

The alternative is for the user to 

* manually list every object in the bucket;
* manually delete every object in the bucket; then
* actually delete the bucket

This is a lot of work for something which takes 2 clicks in the web console. Boto should handle this work for me.

issue_title: Show quantitative progress during deisctl install/start
issue_body:
In the process of installing and starting a Deis cluster via deisctl, it would be nice to see a more quantitative progress of how much of the process is left to go as well as quell questions of "is it even _doing_ anything?" when slower components (like builder) are building/deploying.

Some suggestions that have been thrown out include:
- showing the deisctl logs for the component that's currently being started (obviously this would be verbose and probably a bit too much for first time users, but it'd be awesome to have for automated tests)
- showing a [step N] of [step M] type progress meter
- showing a physical progress bar of some sort

issue_title: Limit max containers per node
issue_body:
Hi, 
I've a lot of containers running with a memory limitation, i.e. 128MB. This restriction is only set in case the process running in the container try to consume more than 128MB, but in a normal situation it only consumes 60MB. 

If I've 1024MB of free memory in my node, Deis will run (1024M / 60M) 17 applications in this node, but it should be (1024M / 128M) 8 applications due to the memory restriction.

So, I would like to reserve 128MB for each container, can I do it? Maybe setting a limit of maximum number of containers per node?

issue_title: Deis v2 Design Document
issue_body:
[Deisv2DesignDocument.pdf](https://github.com/deis/deis/files/10325/Deisv2DesignDocument.pdf)
Here is the link to the current design document for Deis v2:

https://docs.google.com/a/engineyard.com/document/d/1twPhGuLwRXMEtLoyQfx8_PEHt1YAEJKmblXqjTK2VlU/pub

It is backed by a Google Doc, but I don't seem to have the privileges to make comments visible. Feel free to leave comments inline here.

This is a work in progress. While the main concepts are solidifying pretty quickly, the details are all still in flux. We're still trying to iron out the details of how we can keep lots of people involved in the design process. If you have some ideas, feel free to share those here, too.

Here's how we went about writing this document:

- In late July and early August, I put together an initial version. It was more of a rough proposal for how we might switch from Fleet to Kubernetes, and reduce some tech debt along the way
- The core maintainers took a look at it and provided a lot of feedback. @bacongobbler and I made some changes. @aledbf and @carmstrong  made several very helpful comments when I was at this point.
- Over the last two months, @bacongobbler @kmala @smothiki and I did some explorations and I drafted out ideas as we had them. @sgoings and @krancour provided more feedback.
- Last month we introduced the ideas at the public release meeting. The document was taking shape at that point.
- A few days ago, @gabrtv @mboersma and I sat at a whiteboard and hashed out the basic architecture (and the splitting Deis into components). I redrafted accordingly, and prepared the document for release.
- At today's public release, we announced this document, and are looking forward to hearing some feedback.

I'll do my best to keep the document at the link above up to date. If I can figure out how to turn on comments, I will.

UPDAT: Attached as PDF document, since even Google Docs sharing seems disabled on our Google Apps instance.

issue_title: Add a lambda to ensure KMS auth key grants are created for IAM roles
issue_body:
It's possible to create service mappings without the associated IAM role. This is by design so that it's possible to create credentials and map them to a service before its deployed. The issue here is that when the service is deployed, the grants need to exist for the service to get its credentials. It's possible for an end-user to go to the service page and update the grants, but this should be automated if possible.

We can create a scheduled lambda that runs at a user definable interval that will get all grants from the KMS auth key, then compare them against the service mappings. If a service mapping exists, but grants do not, we can check to see if an IAM role exists and create grants if so.

issue_title: Use as a "pure" content-based recommender
issue_body:
I was testing lightfm as a content-based recommender. I was thinking in the starting point of a recommendation system, when users have not rated anything yet, so the data matrix is all zeroes.
I would like to use several features, but, for the sake of simplicity, let's say there is just one feature "color" for both users (that user's favourite color) and items (the item color). 
I think I am doing the right encoding. I am passing matrices with several columns for different colors. Each row has a value of 1 in the appropriate color (right now, items have only one colour and users have only one favourite color) and a value of 0 in the rest of the positions.
I was expecting that if a user likes the color purple, the prediction for that user would score better purple items and thus, they will be on the top of the list when I order the array by scores in descending order. However, I am getting quite random recommendations every time I train the model an ask for a recommendation for the same user. For example if I execute:

```
for i in range(5):
    model = LightFM(loss='warp')
    model.fit(data, user_features=user_features,
              item_features=image_features, num_threads=2)
    scores = model.predict(1, np.arange(image_features.shape[0]), user_features=user_features,
              item_features=image_features)    
    print(np.argsort(-scores)[0:5]+1) #adding one to align with id numbering
```

I get this output

```
[31 29 38 32 40]
[40 24 16 22 25]
[ 8 24  6 36 25]
[20  8  2  1 17]
[ 1 19 39 12  5]
```
But the only purple items are 26, 27 and 28.
Is it possible to use the model this way?

issue_title: Simplify creating fake servers for tests
issue_body: Although we're encouraging use of VCR, there are cases where people want to use fake TChannel servers for the remote service, especially when they want to create specific failure scenarios. Setting up fake servers in Tornado is trivial but sync client users have to manually set up IOLoop threads, etc. We should make it equally simple to do that for sync client users.

issue_title: Support more vmss creation scenarios
issue_body:
Default VMSS Create:
if the user doesn't specify anything, I think that there should be an LB with public IP and inbound NAT rules for ssh/rdp; there should only be 1 pool (size 120); no round-robin rules, ensure subnet supports 120 vms.

Additional VMSS Create options
* do not create public ip
* reference existing Public IP, LB (don't think we need NAT rules or pools as that is part of the existing LB)

issue_title: Service Principal Login should take subscription ID
issue_body:
AAD Servie Principals are hard enough, we should make it easier on users and allow them to login to the CLI with SP Client ID/Secret and the Subscription ID as well as the Tenant ID.

The CLI can retrieve the tenant id in the same way the xplat-cli does it.

issue_title: RiakError with no further details sometimes raised when calling store()
issue_body:
Very occasionally, I'll see a RiakError (with no further details) raised during the execution of my code, typically when calling the `store()` method on a riak object (with no arguments).

The traceback (starting from the line immediately after the `store()` call) looks like this:

```python
  File "/home/ubuntu/env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/riak/riak_object.py", line 286, in store
    timeout=timeout)
  File "/home/ubuntu/env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/riak/client/transport.py", line 196, in wrapper
    return self._with_retries(pool, thunk)
  File "/home/ubuntu/env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/riak/client/transport.py", line 138, in _with_retries
    return fn(transport)
  File "/home/ubuntu/env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/riak/client/transport.py", line 194, in thunk
    return fn(self, transport, *args, **kwargs)

    timeout=timeout)

    MSG_CODE_PUT_RESP)

    return self._recv_msg(expect)

It happens very rarely, and I haven't been able to reproduce it on demand yet to uncover the actual problem. In the meantime, I would appreciate any suggestions on the best way to handle this. Thanks in advance.

issue_title: Do not enable logging by default
issue_body:
The neo4j bolt driver pollutes the console (in my IDE or Jupyter notebook with debug messages). 
 
Logging best practice for libraries dictates that the library does NOT enable al logging handler. See http://docs.python-guide.org/en/latest/writing/logging/#logging-in-a-library and https://docs.python.org/3/howto/logging.html#configuring-logging-for-a-library

Otherwise: document clearly how to silence the debug logging.

In my app, I am able to take a websocket connection in, and forward it through a TCP socket to connect to a VNC session using novncd. issue_title: Invalid host/port specified error on Windows
issue_body:
[We use waitress in devpi](https://bitbucket.org/hpk42/devpi/src/f62e61da0c8716692663fd366c0710dcbc905f04/server/devpi_server/main.py?at=default&fileviewer=file-view-default#main.py-144) like this:
```
  serve(app, host=host, port=port, threads=50)
```
 and on Windows got #138 with waitress 1.0. To work around that I tried to change to the following:
```
  serve(app, listen="%s:%s" % (host, port), threads=50)
```
But then I got ``ValueError: Invalid host/port specified.``

This only happens on Windows, Linux and OS X is fine. For now we pinned to ``waitress<1``.

issue_title: unhandy naming of parameter names in SR830 driver (and possibly others)
issue_body:
I would like to comment on a practice I have noticed in a number of places in the qcodes code base I have some comments on. For instance, the SR830 driver has parameters "chN_ratio", "chN_display", "chN_databuffer", where N is in [1, 2]. 

I do not understand why do we not have ch_ratio(N, ....) etc? What happens if in my experiment I want
to switch from channel 1 to 2? Change all numbers manually? Or what happens if I want to access channels one and two? Copy and paste code literally, instead of having a looping construct? Instead of the channel number being part of the parameter name, this should be an argument with which we call the parameter

This happens not only in this driver but in a number of them, and I do not think we should work this way.  What do you guys think?

We've hardcoded our scipy install command to grab the 1.0 version that works:

As I dug into the test, I discovered that the psi operator was being passed an argument of 0.  The psi function is discontinuous at 0, and could be though of as having either a +inf or -inf value depending on whether 0 is approached from the positive or negative x-axis. issue_title: [OP] LayerNorm in MXNet
issue_body:
## Description

[Layer Normalization](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1607.06450.pdf) is becoming more and more common in deep learning models, especially RNNs. We need to support the layer normalization layer. Although we can implement it by combining the existing symbols, e.g, [Implementation in Sockeye](https://github.com/awslabs/sockeye/blob/master/sockeye/layers.py#L53-L112), the speed and memory cost would be large due to the various broadcasting operators that cannot be calculated inplace. Thus, we need to write our own C++ implementation of this operator.

Some reference implementations:
1. https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/1959
2. https://github.com/MycChiu/fast-LayerNorm-TF

issue_title: airflow_backfill overrides start_date
issue_body:
I ran an `airflow backfill` command as follows:

```
airflow backfill -sd /etc/airflow/dags -s 2014-08-06 -e 2014-08-07 monolith-db-daily
```

My dag (`monolith-db-daily`) had never been run, and had a start_date of datetime.now(). The observed behavior was that the backfill began on 2014-08-02, and continued past 2014-08-07 all the way to present day.

In addition, the DAG was scheduled to run daily at 4am. I observed scheduled execution_dates every day at 4am, for 2014-08-02 through present day, BUT I ALSO observed a second execution on 2014-08-06 and 2014-08-07 at 00:00. I suspect `airflow` backfill is making some assumption about time that's not correct if you schedule daily executions other than 00:00.

issue_title: Install Instructions for .NET Core Runtime 2.1.0-preview1 on Linux Ubuntu 16.04
issue_body:
Following the instructions here to install 2.1.0-preview1 on Ubuntu 16.04 gives me an error when running apt update: https://www.microsoft.com/net/download/linux-package-manager/ubuntu16-04/runtime-2.1.0-preview1

```
# apt update
E: Type 'deb[arch' is not known on line 1 in source list /etc/apt/sources.list.d/dotnetdev.list
E: The list of sources could not be read.
```

This line in the instructions should be changed from
```
sudo sh -c 'echo "deb[arch = amd64] https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/microsoft-ubuntu-xenial-prod xenial main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/dotnetdev.list'
```

to 

```
sudo sh -c 'echo "deb [arch=amd64] https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/microsoft-ubuntu-xenial-prod xenial main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/dotnetdev.list'
```

which adds a space beteween ```deb``` and ```[arch``` and removes the spacing between ```arch = amd64```

Doing this fixed apt and allowed me to install.

issue_title: Add flyway support
Snippet client's _is_app_running() doesn't work properly, leading to race conditions
issue_body:
Slow loading snippets fail because _is_app_running() returns True even when nothing is on the other side of the socket.
This leads to race conditions between the snippet app really starting and the connection attempt.

```
>>> sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
>>> print self._port
8822
>>> result = sock.connect_ex(('127.0.0.1', self._port))
>>> print result
0
And yet...
$ nc 127.0.0.1 8822
$
```

issue_title: Dask wrappers/interface for pyFFTW
issue_body:
Often I use FFTs on large data and am eager to get any speed up I can with it. pyFFTW has been great at delivering really nice performance while also making it very easy to use FFTW thanks to the NumPy style interface.

Though one thing that would be really great is to make it easier still to leverage pyFFTW on data much larger than memory and handle this in parallel. One library that is really fantastic for this is Dask. By allowing one to treat massive files like NumPy arrays and leverage delayed computation, one is able to construct different workflows with large data while keeping focusing on the big picture.

In an attempt to get these two great libraries to play together, @mrocklin, @jcrist, and I worked together on abstracting an FFT interface in Dask ( https://github.com/dask/dask/pull/2093 ) to allow one to easily wrap FFT functions from external libraries that match the NumPy API. While doing this I made sure to test against pyFFTW explicitly. It is trivial for any user to roll these themselves. However, I wondered if it would make sense to included some prewrapped copies of these functions in pyFFTW as part of a new Dask interface for instance.

Would be curious to know your thoughts on this proposal.

issue_title: Add support for write only properties
issue_body:
## Issue description

We have a few cases where we have C++ code that has write only properties (just a set method) that we'd like to promote.  These are basically identical to def_property_readonly except with just a set method.  The current def_property can't be used because it relies on the read function always being there to get docs.  The additions we made are:
- def_property_writeonly
- def_property_writeonly_static

If you don't think this is appropriate, feel free to close this.  Otherwise, I'll submit a PR w/ the updated functions and test cases that we've implemented.  If you think it's necessary, I'm sure we could also add def_writeonly and def_writeonly_static for completeness.

issue_title: Numexpr 3 language scope
issue_body:
Do you have a document specifying the names of all the functions and operators in numexpr 3? (Including constants like `e` and `pi`: do you recognize these names or do they have to be in scope? In aggressive mode, which powers do you replace with explicit multiplication? Etc.)

I'm developing a histogramming package (https://github.com/diana-hep/histbook) that takes expressions as quantities to evaluate and plot (could address your #205). These expressions look a lot like numexpr and could benefit from being exactly the same language as numexpr (my #22 and #23). For instance, I should rename my "asin" to "arcsin" for complete compatibility.

But I see that you're in preparation for a big release, so I should probably aim for compatibility with the new version. Do you have a specification?

Thanks!

issue_title: Disable python setup.py install?
issue_body:
We have had a lot of trouble having versions of sos packages installed by `python setup.py install` and `pip install sos`. Basically, `pip` installs straight into `site-packages` with directories such as

```
sos/
sos-0.9.13.2.dist-info/
```

and `python setup.py install` would install

```
sos-0.9.13.3-py3.6.egg/
```

and which one will be called under which condition has been a mystery.

The good news is that `pip install .` supports installing from local source directory and installs to

```
sos/
sos-0.9.13.3-py3.6.egg-info/
```
so that subsequent `pip install sos` or `pip install .` will write to the same directory `sos`. That is to say, we should encourage `pip install .` and `discourage `python setup.py install`.

The problem must have been noticed by the Python community and I just noticed that I cannot run `python setup.py install` for jupyter, whose [`setup.py`](https://github.com/jupyter/nbconvert/blob/master/setup.py)

has the following lines of code

```

from setuptools.command.bdist_egg import bdist_egg

class bdist_egg_disabled(bdist_egg):
    """Disabled version of bdist_egg
 
    Prevents setup.py install performing setuptools' default easy_install,
    which it should never ever do.
    """
    def run(self):
        sys.exit("Aborting implicit building of eggs. Use `pip install .` to install from source.")

cmdclass['bdist_egg'] = bdist_egg if 'bdist_egg' in sys.argv else bdist_egg_disabled

```
and running `python setup.py install` will trigger the error.

```
$ python setup.py install
running install
running bdist_egg
Aborting implicit building of eggs. Use `pip install .` to install from source.
```

I think we should follow this.

issue_title: ZMQStream.on_recv/on_send should pass stream to callbacks
issue_body:
Currently, the on_recv/on_send system only passes the *message* to the callback, but they should really pass both the stream and the message.  This is the way the czmq reactor behaves, and it makes it easier to use the same callback with multiple streams.

The only tricky thing here is how to add this in a sensible way that does not break current apps that only expect the one argument.

For now, this behavior can be emulated with closures and intermediary functions:

```python
def handle_recv(stream, msg):
    dostuff()

for s in streams:
    s.on_recv(lambda msg: handle_recv(s,msg))
```

But this is suboptimal.

issue_title: Timeouts for post_to_esp()
issue_body:
We recently had networking troubles which lead to certain (Sendgrid) SSL handshakes stalling forever. This lead all our mail sending workers to eventually stall and lock up all background tasks.

Usually, for other HTTPS requests, we add a (generous) timeout (see http://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#timeouts). The timeout is chosen such that it only triggers in those rare, extreme circumstances. We mostly use 30 seconds, but Stripe, as another example, uses even 80 seconds (https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python/blob/f948b8b95b6df5b57c7444a05d6c83c8c5e6a0ac/stripe/http_client.py#L97).

I think anymail should have a default timeout, too. Doesn't even have to be configurable, and the exception could be passed straight to user code.

issue_title: Add support for local .git repositories not yet published to github that are on Windows.
issue_body:
Currently I can ``pip install --upgrade codecov`` on windows.

However I would like to be able to check the coverage of my latest commits locally so I know weather or not I need to add my tests for some test cases before  I put the repository to github. Is there a way to do this? I can do ``"%SystemDrive%\Python360\python.exe" setup.py test`` that runs pytest and passes the one test the local repository has. I just would like to use this to see how many lines are covered locally and then print the % of the code that is covered.

issue_title: Parler combined with Polymorphic and Django-1.7
issue_body:
Today I tried to upgrade to Django-1.7. Unfortunately this does not work, and I have no clue why. I then boiled my project down to a very simply Django installation and am still able to reproduce it.

My environment:
Django==1.7.1
django-parler==1.2.1
django-polymorphic==0.6
...and unimportant stuff...

in settings.py:
```
INSTALLED_APPS = (
    'polymorphic',
    'django.contrib.auth',
    'django.contrib.contenttypes',
    'django.contrib.sessions',
    'django.contrib.admin',
    'django.contrib.staticfiles',
    'parler',
    'my_demo',
)
```

Then I used the demo model.py from the docs:
```
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from django.db import models
from django.utils.encoding import python_2_unicode_compatible, force_text
from parler.models import TranslatableModel, TranslatedFields
from polymorphic import PolymorphicModel
from parler.managers import TranslatableManager

class Product(PolymorphicModel):
    code = models.CharField(blank=False, default='', max_length=16)
    price = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, default=0.00)

@python_2_unicode_compatible
class Pen(TranslatableModel, Product):
    default_manager = TranslatableManager()

    translations = TranslatedFields(
        identifier=models.CharField(blank=False, default='', max_length=255)
    )

    def __str__(self):
        return force_text(self.identifier)
```

but I'am not able to even bootstrap this demo app:
```
   (... snip - bootstrapping ...)
  File ".../django/db/models/base.py", line 299, in add_to_class
    value.contribute_to_class(cls, name)
  File ".../parler/models.py", line 195, in contribute_to_class
    create_translations_model(cls, name, self.meta, **self.fields)
  File ".../parler/models.py", line 158, in create_translations_model
    translations_model = TranslatedFieldsModelBase(name, (TranslatedFieldsModel,), attrs)
  File ".../parler/models.py", line 677, in __new__
    new_class = super(TranslatedFieldsModelBase, mcs).__new__(mcs, name, bases, attrs)
  File ".../django/db/models/base.py", line 170, in __new__
    new_class.add_to_class(obj_name, obj)
  File ".../django/db/models/base.py", line 299, in add_to_class
    value.contribute_to_class(cls, name)
  File ".../django/db/models/fields/related.py", line 1585, in contribute_to_class
    super(ForeignObject, self).contribute_to_class(cls, name, virtual_only=virtual_only)
  File ".../django/db/models/fields/related.py", line 272, in contribute_to_class
    add_lazy_relation(cls, self, other, resolve_related_class)

    operation(field, model, cls)

    field.do_related_class(model, cls)

    self.set_attributes_from_rel()

    self.rel.set_field_name()

    self.field_name = self.field_name or self.to._meta.pk.name
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'name'
```

BTW, the same happens if I use the ``Book`` example.


I saw that ``polymorphic/query.py`` attempts to handle something:

```

issue_title: CoordinateEncoder not compatible with float radii
issue_body:
The docstrings for `CoordinateEncoder._neighbors()` and `CoordinateEncoder.encodeIntoArray()` specify `float` values for radius, but using floats result in exceptions.  This is due to an implementation detail in `_neighbors()` where radius is added to values in the coordinate, forcing a cast to float in the second argument of `range()`.  For example, the following code snippet produces a `TypeError` exception:

```
CoordinateEncoder().encode((numpy.array([1]), 3.0))
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/Users/amarshall/nta/nupic/src/nupic/encoders/base.py", line 133, in encode
    self.encodeIntoArray(inputData, output)
  File "/Users/amarshall/nta/nupic/src/nupic/encoders/coordinate.py", line 109, in encodeIntoArray
    neighbors = self._neighbors(coordinate, radius)
  File "/Users/amarshall/nta/nupic/src/nupic/encoders/coordinate.py", line 130, in _neighbors
    ranges = [range(n-radius, n+radius+1) for n in coordinate.tolist()]
TypeError: range() integer end argument expected, got float.
```

I think corrective action should be to fix documentation to reflect the correct type and possibly assert on type in `CoordinateEncoder.encodeIntoArray()`

issue_title: linear algebra/matrix libraries rundown, benchmark
issue_body:
after https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/372 we should 
reimplement it in considered libraries, and see which/iff performs better than current state. 

To choose correctly we can:

* 1/ reimplement https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/372 and `benchmark` - This might be very difficult, time consuming, costly to get it all right. 
* 2/ do theoretical analysis of the type of problem being done (eg sparse matrix summation, small fixed sized vector multiplication, sine over a vector) and make an educated guess based on current benchmarks published by the libraries.
* 3/ let concurrent solutions evolve and choose the best -- I'm not sure many people would buy this. Due to the complexity and as we don't have any such solution so far.

issue_body: Loren has suggested that we update the parameter to something like source_driver which will accept a value from an accepted list (including s3, http, and so on) and we use that to identify the source of downloads, etc.  Sounds like a good plan to me as I think it broadens the capabilities of Watchmaker.

issue_title: Non-Maximum Supression on the GPU
issue_body: Is there any interest in an NMS layer that runs on the GPU for torchvision? I have one implemented; it gives a 1-2 order of magnitude speedup over a naive version composed from pytorch ops. Would be happy to contribute it if anyone's interested.

issue_title: Re-use of calculated resampling parameters
issue_body:
At the moment satpy doesn't re-use the calculated resampling parameters for the following channels with identical fingerprints if the cache_dir kwarg is not given. With cache_dir set, the resampling parameters are written to disk the first time, and then **reloaded** for each of the matching datasets.

My suggestion is to find a way to resample the datasets in such order that all matching datasets are processed consecutively and the resampling parameters could be kept in-memory for that duration. In this way the parameters would not need to be reloaded (cache_dir set) or recalculated (cache_dir not set) for each dataset. The extra time used is even more pronounced when using bilinear sampling and/or large areas.

For testing:

`# Set debugging messages on
from satpy.utils import debug_on
debug_on()
# Create a scene from some files
glbl = Scene(filenames=fnames)
# Load all available datasets
glbl.load(glbl.available_dataset_names())
# Resample without cache. Note that the resampling is recalculated for each dataset
lcl = glbl.resample('euron1')
# Resample with cache. Note that the resampling parameters are reloaded, if already calculated
# and saved, for each dataset
lcl = glbl.resample('euron1', cache_dir='/tmp')
`

issue_title: Add alpha channel to Colormaps
issue_body: New versions of trollmage should allow and use an alpha channel as part of the Colormap.  Current color maps allow (r,g,b).  It is suggested that this be extended to (r,g,b,a) and the alpha channel would be used when colorizing and palletizing imagery.

issue_title: NLU config should not allow  unknown_word_prob > 0  unknown_words_replacement_string = null
issue_body:
## Bug description

- define a NLU config with:  `unknown_word_prob = 0.1` and `unknown_words_replacement_string = null`
- fit an NLU engine with it -> `TypeError: object of type 'NoneType' has no len()`

## Suggested fix

Do not allow such a setup, warn the user

issue_title: Item ids must be sequential starting at zero
issue_body:
It was non-obvious to me that the integer ids supplied to add_item() must be sequential and start at zero. But if they are not, get_n_items() returns bogus counts. Is this the intended contract?  It seems like if the ids are simply increasing sequential integers that AnnoyIndex would manage incrementing the ids.  Why have to specify it in add_item() at all? Am I missing something?

For example, this appears to work:
    >>> t = annoy.AnnoyIndex(3)
    >>> t.add_item(0, [1, 2, 3])
    >>> t.add_item(1, [2, 2, 3])
    >>> t.add_item(2, [3, 2, 3])
    >>> t.build(1)
    True
    >>> t.get_n_items()
    3

But use arbitrary ids, and the counts are tricked:
    >>> t = annoy.AnnoyIndex(3)
    >>> t.add_item(1, [1, 2, 3])
    >>> t.build(1)
    True
    >>> t.get_n_items()
    2

Or:
    >>>
    >>> t = annoy.AnnoyIndex(3)
    >>> t.add_item(100, [1, 2, 3])
    >>> t.build(1)
    True
    >>> t.get_n_items()
    101

issue_title: Why is configuration file called 'client.cfg'
issue_body:
Maybe a stupid question, but why is the configuration file called `client.cfg`, when it typically contains non-client (e.g. scheduler) stuff as well?

Wouldn't it be nicer to have at least some hint of "luigi" in the name, e.g. `luigi.cfg`?

Can be handy when you use the "use config from current working dir" from a directory with loads of other configs and files.

issue_title: Support shared secrets instead of passwords in conf file
issue_body:
We need to support a more secure way to supply passwords than putting them in the conf file.  Here's my proposal:

1. support shared secrets configuration, such as Kubernetes secrets
2. add a new key, defaulting to "conf" when not present:

    secrets=[conf,kubernetes,vault,...?]

3. add a new subtree to the config for each secret config, e.g.:

    kubernetes-secrets:
       secretname: 
       secretdir:

4. Add a flag for each password entry to indicate the key in the secrets:

       superuser:
          username: #supername
          password: #superpassword

Questions:

* does this make sense?
* what token can we use to indicate a shared secret lookup key?

Notes:

* I will do the implementation of the above for Kubernetes.  Implementing it for other shared secret tools will be up to someone else.

issue_title: Validate service IDs
issue_body:
This avoids conflict between user-defined service ID's, and auto-assigned service ID's when no datastore object is found for controller ID.

(Default behavior here is to convert the list of ints (controller ID) to a '-' joined string)

Example: [1,2,3] becomes '1-2-3'

issue_title: “CustomizedSegmentor: Model not loaded!”问题（Python 3.6.1 | Anaconda 4.4.0 win7 64bit）
issue_body:
@endyul 您好！最近在使用pyltp个性化分词功能，遇到了`“CustomizedSegmentor: Model not loaded!”`问题，代码如下所示。经调试确认，代码是在执行代码`“CustomizedSegmentor.segment(str)”`时报出上述错误。请教一下，该如何解决？

```python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import os
from pyltp import CustomizedSegmentor

LTP_DATA_DIR = './'  # ltp模型目录的路径
cws_model_path = os.path.join(LTP_DATA_DIR, 'cws.model')  # 分词模型路径，模型名称为`cws.model`
custom_model_path = os.path.join(LTP_DATA_DIR, 'selfcws-30.model')

customized_segmentor = CustomizedSegmentor()  # 初始化实例
customized_segmentor.load(cws_model_path, custom_model_path)
words = customized_segmentor.segment('亚硝酸盐是一种化学物质')
print ('\t'.join(words))
customized_segmentor.release()
```
运行环境如下所示：
> - 操作系统：win7 64位
> - 安装环境：Python 3.6.1 | Anaconda 4.4.0(64-bit)
> - pyltp版本：0.1.9.1

另外，在linux环境下pyltp个性化分词可以正常运行，使用模型文件、pyltp版本和win7环境下完全一致。

issue_title: Can't override `type="search"` for text field anymore
issue_body:
Hi!

I'm migrating my project from Django 1.9 to 1.11. I have the following code which was working great in Django 1.9 but doesn't work in Django 1.11:

```html
{% render_field form.q type='search' required='required' class='term-input' %}
```

And here's my Django form:

```py
class SearchForm(forms.Form):
    q = forms.CharField(label='Search')
```

Only if I explicitly override the field widget's `type` atribute - it works:

```py
class SearchForm(forms.Form):
    q = forms.CharField(label='Search', widget=TextInput(attrs={'type': 'search'}))
```

## Investigation

AFAIU, Django 1.11 has moved widgets rendering from Python code into templates (which is 100% positive change IMO), but now they treat `type` attribute in a special way:

```
{# django-1.11.7/django/forms/templates/django/forms/widgets/input.html #}
<input type="{{ widget.type }}" name="{{ widget.name }}"{% if widget.value != None %} value="{{ widget.value|stringformat:'s' }}"{% endif %}{% include "django/forms/widgets/attrs.html" %} />
```

So when I override 
```
{% render_field form.q required='required' class='term-input' data-x=1 %}
``` 
... - everything works great, but not when I change `type` :(

issue_title: extracting device serial number
issue_body:
The device serial numbers from FTDI chips are being extracted incorrectly on windows systems. There is an added "A" in the end of the serial number. I tested this with several FTDI adapters and using their provided FT_Prog to confirm the serial numbers. On a linux system, the results are correct. With an ATMEGA16u2 chip, the reported serial number is correct though.

https://github.com/pyserial/pyserial/issues/331Under Raspberry Pi 3B+
/dev/ttyUSB1 (USB VID=0403) Serial#:=AJ02WA8P

Under Windows 10
COM14 (USB VID=0403) Serial#:=AJ02WA8PA

issue_title: Incomplete list of supported Space.update() operations in the manual
issue_body:
The manual says:
```
The following update operations are supported by Tarantool:
    • ``'='`` – assign new value to the field
    • ``'+'`` – add argument to the field (*both arguments are treated as signed 32-bit ints*)
    • ``'^'`` – bitwise AND (*only for 32-bit integers*)
    • ``'|'`` – bitwise XOR (*only for 32-bit integers*)
    • ``'&'`` – bitwise OR  (*only for 32-bit integers*)
    • ``'splice'`` – implementation of `Perl splice <http://perldoc.perl.org/functions/splice.html>`_ function
```
The above is missing the following supported operations: '-' (subtraction), '#' (delete) and '!' (insert).

issue_title: Trait Chaining is not working as mentioned in the docs
issue_body:
The Trait Chaining is not working as mentioned in the docs or as it should. When you chain a trait, the parent trait values actually override the child one

For eg

```
class OrderFactory(factory.Factory):
    class Meta:
        model = Order

    # Can be pending/shipping/received
    state = 'pending'
    shipped_on = None
    shipped_by = None
    received_on = None
    received_by = None

    class Params:
        shipped = factory.Trait(
            state='shipped',
            shipped_on=datetime.date.today,
            shipped_by=factory.SubFactory(EmployeeFactory),
        )
        received = factory.Trait(
            shipped=True,
            state='received',
            shipped_on=datetime.date.today - datetime.timedelta(days=4),
            received_on=datetime.date.today,
            received_by=factory.SubFactory(CustomerFactory),
        )
```
In this example borrowed from documentation following assert should work

```
o = OrderFactory.build(received=True)
assert o.state == 'received' 
```
However this is not the case with the latest factory boy version ie `2.8.1`. The value of o.state evaluates to `shipped`.
